SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.automation

Interface IAutomationHub

All Superinterfaces:
Runnable
All Known Implementing Classes:
AutomationHub
public interface IAutomationHub extends Runnable
Interface for the Smart Home Automation Hub.

The Automation Hub is a standalone component responsible for:

  • Storing user-defined automation rules, loaded from StateStore on startup and kept in sync via the AUTOMATION topic.
  • Evaluating incoming DeviceEvents against all active rules and publishing Commands to the COMMAND topic for matches.

Threading model

The hub uses a 2-thread model:

  • Netty receiver thread — managed by MessageBusClientRunner. Deserialises inbound frames and places Message objects on the inbound queue via enqueue(Message).
  • Main processing thread — runs run(), blocks on the inbound queue, and dispatches each message to either handleEvent(DeviceEvent) or handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage) based on topic.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    addRule(AutomationRule rule)
    Adds a new automation rule to the in-memory registry.
    void
    enqueue(Message<?> message)
    Places an inbound message on the processing queue.
    List<AutomationRule>
    getAllRules()
    Returns all rules currently held by this hub, including inactive ones.
    boolean
    handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage message)
    Applies an automation rule operation (ADD, REMOVE, or TOGGLE) received from the AUTOMATION topic.
    void
    handleEvent(DeviceEvent event)
    Evaluates a DeviceEvent against all active rules and publishes a Command to the COMMAND topic for each match.
    boolean
    removeRule(String ruleId)
    Removes an existing automation rule by ID.
    void
    run()
    Main processing loop.
    void
    start()
    Starts the Automation Hub.
    void
    stop()
    Stops the hub's processing loop.
    boolean
    toggleRule(String ruleId, boolean active)
    Enables or disables an existing rule without removing it.

  • Method Details

    • start

      void start()
      Starts the Automation Hub.

      In order:

      1. Loads all existing rules from StateStore so the hub is immediately ready without waiting for the coordinator to replay rules.
      2. Sends REGISTER and SUBSCRIBE frames for the EVENT and AUTOMATION topics to the Message Bus via MessageBusClientRunner.

    • stop

      void stop()
      Stops the hub's processing loop. The main thread exits after the current message finishes processing. In-flight messages remaining in the queue are discarded.

    • enqueue

      void enqueue(Message<?> message)
      Places an inbound message on the processing queue. Called by the Netty pipeline handler on the receiver thread — returns immediately so the I/O thread is never blocked.
      Parameters:
      message - the message received from the Message Bus

    • run

      void run()
      Main processing loop. Blocks on the inbound queue and dispatches each message by topic:
      • TopicType.EVENT → handleEvent(DeviceEvent)
      • TopicType.AUTOMATION → handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage)
      Exits when stop() is called or the thread is interrupted.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • addRule

      boolean addRule(AutomationRule rule)
      Adds a new automation rule to the in-memory registry. Called by handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage) for ADD operations.
      Parameters:
      rule - the rule to add; ignored if null

    • removeRule

      boolean removeRule(String ruleId)
      Removes an existing automation rule by ID. Has no effect if no rule with the given ID exists. Called by handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage) for REMOVE operations.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to remove

    • toggleRule

      boolean toggleRule(String ruleId, boolean active)
      Enables or disables an existing rule without removing it. Called by handleAutomationMessage(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRuleMessage) for TOGGLE operations.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - the ID of the rule to update
      active - true to activate, false to deactivate

    • getAllRules

      List<AutomationRule> getAllRules()
      Returns all rules currently held by this hub, including inactive ones.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable snapshot of the current rule list

    • handleEvent

      void handleEvent(DeviceEvent event)
      Evaluates a DeviceEvent against all active rules and publishes a Command to the COMMAND topic for each match.

      A rule matches when all three conditions hold:

      1. rule.triggerDeviceType == event.sourceDeviceType
      2. rule.triggerEventType.equals(event.eventType)
      3. rule.triggerDeviceId == null (wildcard) or rule.triggerDeviceId.equals(event.sourceDeviceId)
      Parameters:
      event - the device event to evaluate

    • handleAutomationMessage

      boolean handleAutomationMessage(AutomationRuleMessage message)
      Applies an automation rule operation (ADD, REMOVE, or TOGGLE) received from the AUTOMATION topic.

      Delegates to addRule(edu.yu.cs.com3810.models.AutomationRule), removeRule(java.lang.String), or toggleRule(java.lang.String, boolean) based on AutomationRuleMessage.getOperation().

      Parameters:
      message - the automation rule operation to apply
